page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 16 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 128.0 GB of RAM, providing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. It lacks local storage, requiring external storage attachments, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps.

Benchmark evaluations place this instance in the average performance tier across most metrics. The Intel Xeon processor delivers a single-core stress-ng score of 1436.39 and a multi-core score of 15326.6. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block bandwidth of 853.84 GB/sec and a large block write bandwidth of 49.06 GB/sec. For specialized tasks, it achieves a Redis SET throughput of 14.91 million operations per second and a static web serving throughput of 12.32 GB/sec.

While the instance handles smaller language models adequately, processing 937.39 prompt tokens per second on a 135M model, its performance drops to a weak rating of 23.2 tokens per second for 7B model prompt processing. This indicates a clear tradeoff when deploying larger AI workloads. Given its high memory density and dedicated CPU allocation, the ecs.hfr6.4xlarge is well-suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, caching layers like Redis, and general-purpose enterprise workloads that do not require local NVMe storage or high-speed baseline networking.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.hfr6.4xlarge is a memory-optimized virtual server instance utilizing the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 16 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 8269CY processor running at 3.1 GHz, paired with 128.0 GB of RAM. This configuration yields a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The instance does not include local storage and operates with a baseline network bandwidth of 5 Gbps. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 15326.6 and a Redis SET throughput of 14.91 million operations per second. However, it exhibits weaker performance on larger workloads, such as 7B LLM prompt processing. This instance is best suited for memory-intensive applications, database operations, and caching systems that require dedicated compute resources but do not depend on local NVMe storage.
